questions.question: A farmer wants to fence a rectangular area of 1800 ft¬≤ and divide it into three equal pens using fencing parallel to one side of the rectangle. Let L be the length of the side parallel to the dividing fences, and W be the other side. Which expression gives the total amount of fencing needed, and what value of L minimizes it?
questions.explanation: Area: L√óW=1800 ‚Üí W=1800/L. Fencing: 2 lengths L (top/bottom) + 4 widths W (2 outer sides + 2 dividers). So F=2L+4W=2L+7200/L. Minimize: F'(L)=2‚ąí7200/L¬≤=0 ‚Üí L¬≤=3600 ‚Üí L=60 ft.

questions.question: Find the area of the largest rectangle that can be inscribed in an equilateral triangle with side length 10, where one side of the rectangle lies on the base of the triangle. The rectangle has symmetric corners at coordinates (¬Īa,0) and (¬Īa,y), with the top corners touching the triangle's sides.
questions.explanation: Triangle height=5‚ąö3. By similar triangles, y=‚ąö3a. Rectangle width=10‚ąí2a, height=y=‚ąö3a. Area A=(10‚ąí2a)(‚ąö3a)=‚ąö3(10a‚ąí2a¬≤). A'(a)=‚ąö3(10‚ąí4a)=0 ‚Üí a=2.5. Width=5, height=2.5‚ąö3 ‚Üí Area=12.5‚ąö3.

questions.question: A right circular cone is inscribed in a sphere of radius 5. If the cone's axis passes through the sphere's center, find the cone height h that maximizes the cone's volume.
questions.explanation: Let cone base be at y=c, vertex at y=5. Radius r satisfies r¬≤=25‚ąíc¬≤, height h=5‚ąíc. Volume V=ŌÄ/3(25‚ąíc¬≤)(5‚ąíc). Substitute c=5‚ąíh: V=ŌÄ/3(10h‚ąíh¬≤)h=ŌÄ/3(10h¬≤‚ąíh¬≥). V'(h)=ŌÄ/3(20h‚ąí3h¬≤)=0 ‚Üí h=20/3 (h‚Č†0).

questions.question: A rain gutter is formed by bending up both edges of a 12-inch wide aluminum sheet through 90¬į. If the bent-up edges have height x inches, what value of x maximizes the cross-sectional area (and thus water capacity) of the gutter?
questions.explanation: After bending, base width=12‚ąí2x, side height=x. Cross-section area A=x(12‚ąí2x)=12x‚ąí2x¬≤. A'(x)=12‚ąí4x=0 ‚Üí x=3. Check endpoints: x=0 or 6 gives A=0, so x=3 maximizes.

questions.question: A rectangular page contains 96 square inches of printed material. The margins are 1 inch at top and bottom, and 0.5 inch on each side. Let w be the width of the printed region. Find w that minimizes the total area of the page.
questions.explanation: Printed height h=96/w. Page width=w+1, page height=h+2. Area A=(w+1)(96/w+2)=98+2w+96/w. A'(w)=2‚ąí96/w¬≤=0 ‚Üí w¬≤=48 ‚Üí w=4‚ąö3.

questions.question: A window is constructed from a rectangle topped by an isosceles triangle whose base equals the rectangle's width. Total perimeter is 20 ft. If the rectangle has width 2b and height h, and the triangle has height t, which equation correctly relates b and t for maximum area, and what is b?
questions.explanation: Perimeter: 2h+2b+2‚ąö(b¬≤+t¬≤)=20. Area A=2bh+bt. Optimize t for fixed b: ‚ąāA/‚ąāt=b‚ąí2bt/‚ąö(b¬≤+t¬≤)=0 ‚Üí t=b/‚ąö3. Substitute back: A(b)=20b‚ąíb¬≤(2+‚ąö3). A'(b)=20‚ąí2b(2+‚ąö3)=0 ‚Üí b=10/(2+‚ąö3)=10(2‚ąí‚ąö3).

questions.question: A power station is on the north bank of a 1 km wide river. A factory is 4 km downstream on the south bank. Cable costs $10 per m underwater and $8 per m on land. If the cable goes underwater from the power station to a point x km downstream on the south bank, then overland to the factory, find x that minimizes cost.
questions.explanation: Underwater distance=‚ąö(1+x¬≤) km, land distance=4‚ąíx km. Cost C(x)=10000‚ąö(1+x¬≤)+8000(4‚ąíx) in dollars (or scale by 1000). Minimize: C'(x)=10000x/‚ąö(1+x¬≤)‚ąí8000=0 ‚Üí 10x=8‚ąö(1+x¬≤) ‚Üí x=4/3.

questions.question: A trapezoid is inscribed in a semicircle of radius 2, with its longer base along the diameter. If the trapezoid's shorter base is parallel to the diameter at height y above it, find the maximum possible area of the trapezoid.
questions.explanation: Long base=4, short base length=2‚ąö(4‚ąíy¬≤), height=y. Area A=¬Ĺ(4+2‚ąö(4‚ąíy¬≤))y=2y+y‚ąö(4‚ąíy¬≤). A'(y)=2+‚ąö(4‚ąíy¬≤)‚ąíy¬≤/‚ąö(4‚ąíy¬≤)=0 ‚Üí ‚ąö(4‚ąíy¬≤)=1 ‚Üí y=‚ąö3 ‚Üí A=2‚ąö3+‚ąö3=3‚ąö3.

questions.question: A cylindrical can must hold 1000 cm¬≥ of liquid. Material costs: side $0.02/cm¬≤, top $0.03/cm¬≤, bottom $0.04/cm¬≤. If the can has radius r and height h, which cost function C(r) is correct, and what condition on r minimizes cost?
questions.explanation: Volume: ŌÄr¬≤h=1000 ‚Üí h=1000/(ŌÄr¬≤). Cost: side=2ŌÄrh√ó0.02=0.04ŌÄrh=40/r; top=0.03ŌÄr¬≤; bottom=0.04ŌÄr¬≤; sum=40/r+0.07ŌÄr¬≤. C'(r)=‚ąí40/r¬≤+0.14ŌÄr=0 ‚Üí 0.14ŌÄr¬≥=40 ‚Üí r¬≥=2000/(7ŌÄ).

questions.question: A cone is formed by cutting out a sector of central angle őł (radians) from a circular piece of paper of radius 12 cm and joining the edges. Find őł that maximizes the cone's volume.
questions.explanation: Cone base circumference=12őł ‚Üí radius r=6őł/ŌÄ. Slant height=12 ‚Üí height h=‚ąö(144‚ąí36őł¬≤/ŌÄ¬≤). Volume V=(ŌÄ/3)r¬≤h=(12őł¬≤/ŌÄ)‚ąö(144‚ąí36őł¬≤/ŌÄ¬≤). Let k=őł/ŌÄ, maximize k¬≤‚ąö(144‚ąí36k¬≤): gives k¬≤=8/3 ‚Üí őł=ŌÄ‚ąö(8/3)=2ŌÄ‚ąö6/3.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent work ‚ÄĒ you've mastered complex optimization setup and solving.
scoreMessages.70: Very good ‚ÄĒ you understand the concepts but review geometry relationships.
scoreMessages.50: Fair ‚ÄĒ focus on translating word problems into mathematical functions.
scoreMessages.0: Needs practice ‚ÄĒ start with drawing diagrams and defining variables clearly.
